plan.action: Verify lib/lock.sh already implements all required functions: lock_acquire (with force override), lock_release, lock_exists, lock_path. Confirm it writes PID to LOCK file, prints ERROR with --force-lock hint on conflict, and prints WARNING on forced override.

Verify clippy.sh already sources lib/lock.sh, parses --force-lock flag into FORCE_LOCK variable, calls lock_acquire before starting the loop (exiting with code 1 on failure), and installs a trap to call lock_release on EXIT.

Verify tests/lock.bats covers all acceptance criteria: LOCK created on acquire, PID written, failure when LOCK exists, ERROR message printed, --force-lock hint in output, force override succeeds with WARNING, release removes file, release is idempotent, lock_exists return codes, lock_path output.
Run the full test suite (bats tests/lock.bats) and confirm all tests pass. If any fail, fix the discrepancy in lib/lock.sh or tests/lock.bats.
Run shellcheck on lib/lock.sh and clippy.sh to confirm typecheck passes.

testing_recommendations: Run: bats tests/lock.bats — all 12 existing tests must pass
Ensure lock_acquire creates LOCK with PID content
Ensure lock_acquire returns non-zero and prints ERROR + --force-lock hint when LOCK exists and force=false
Ensure lock_acquire with force=true removes old LOCK, creates new one, and prints WARNING
Ensure lock_release removes LOCK and returns 0 when LOCK absent (idempotent)
Integration: confirm clippy.sh main() calls lock_acquire before loop_run and trap releases on exit
Run shellcheck on lib/lock.sh and clippy.sh with no errors
risks: Implementation appears already complete — main risk is that a test or shellcheck check is failing, causing the ticket to be marked passes:false. Run tests to confirm current status before making any changes.
Trap-based lock_release may not fire on SIGKILL; this is an accepted limitation of file-based locking and does not need to be fixed for this ticket.
